Theorem jca 519
Description: Deduce conjunction of the consequents of two implications ("join consequents with 'and'"). Deduction form of pm3.2 473 and pm3.2i 474. Its associated deduction is jcad 520. Equivalent to the natural deduction rule ∧ I (∧ introduction), see natded 30602. (Contributed by NM, 3-Jan-1993.) (Proof shortened by Wolf Lammen, 25-Oct-2012.)
Hypotheses
Ref Expression
jca.1 ⊢ (𝜑 → 𝜓)
jca.2 ⊢ (𝜑 → 𝜒)
Assertion
Ref Expression
jca ⊢ (𝜑 → (𝜓 ∧ 𝜒))
Proof of Theorem jca
StepHypRef Expression
1 jca.1 . 2 ⊢ (𝜑 → 𝜓)
2 jca.2 . 2 ⊢ (𝜑 → 𝜒)
3 pm3.2 473 . 2 ⊢ (𝜓 → (𝜒 → (𝜓 ∧ 𝜒)))
41, 2, 3sylc 65 1 ⊢ (𝜑 → (𝜓 ∧ 𝜒))
Colors of variables: wff setvar class
Syntax hints:   → wi 4   ∧ wa 399
This theorem was proved from axioms:  ax-mp 5  ax-1 6  ax-2 7  ax-3 8
